title: quantum-genesis — Package 24: Qubit Decoherence as UTAC Threshold System
description: quantum-genesis models superconducting qubit decoherence as a Unified Threshold Adaptive Criticality (UTAC) system, coupling the Quantum Error Correction (QEC) threshold theorem to the CREP (Coherence–Resonance–Emergence–Poetics) framework. The QEC threshold (p_th ≈ 10⁻³) maps to the UTAC phase transition point H*. Calibrated against Google Willow 2024 (below-threshold QEC), IBM Quantum T1 data, npj Quantum Information 2026 (R² > 0.96 for T1 from topology), and Vijay & Lee 2026 (information critical phase in decohered Toric codes). Central result: Γ_quantum ≈ 0.050, placing qubits as the most fragile UTAC system in the GenesisAeon Entropy Atlas, just above solar magnetic fields (P21, Γ ≈ 0.014). Part of the GenesisAeon Entropy Atlas (Packages 17–30).
notes: Synthetic mode (seed=42) requires no quantum hardware. Optional: pip install stim qiskit-ibm-runtime for hardware/simulation backends.
